description:
You are a security engineer who has been asked to rotate an exposed API credential that still sits in a development repository.

CURRENT SYSTEM STATE (before you start):
1. Directory structure:
   /home/user/project
2. File that still contains the leaked credential:
   /home/user/project/.env
3. Contents of /home/user/project/.env (exactly four lines):
   # Application environment variables
   SERVICE_URL="https://dev.example.com"
   OLD_API_KEY="ABC123"
   DEBUG=true

OBJECTIVE:
1. Replace the exposed value ABC123 with the new value XYZ789 inside /home/user/project/.env so that OLD_API_KEY becomes NEW_API_KEY.
   After the change, the four-line file must read exactly:
   # Application environment variables
   SERVICE_URL="https://dev.example.com"
   NEW_API_KEY="XYZ789"
   DEBUG=true

2. Perform a quick scan in /home/user/project (sub-directories included, if any) to verify that the old value ABC123 no longer exists anywhere inside that directory tree.

3. Produce a **rotation verification log** file at:
/home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation_log.txt

   3a. If /home/user/rotation_logs does not yet exist, create it with default user permissions.

   3b. The log file must contain exactly **two lines** and no trailing blank lines.  
   Use the fixed timestamp 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z in both lines (do NOT generate the current time—this fixed value is how the test will decide that your format is correct).

   3c. The required line-by-line format is:
   <timestamp>|<status>|<item>

      • timestamp  : the literal string 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z  
      • status     : UPDATED for successful replacement, NOT_FOUND for proof that the old key no longer appears  
      • item       : 
          – For the UPDATED line, put the full path of the file you changed.  
          – For the NOT_FOUND line, put the literal string OLD_API_KEY

   Hence the finished file /home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation_log.txt must read exactly:

   2024-01-01T00:00:00Z|UPDATED|/home/user/project/.env
   2024-01-01T00:00:00Z|NOT_FOUND|OLD_API_KEY

SUCCESS CRITERIA (checked by automated tests):
• /home/user/project/.env exists and matches the four-line content shown above, byte-for-byte.  
• /home/user/project contains no files that still hold the string ABC123.  
• /home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation_log.txt exists, is readable by the current user, contains exactly the two lines shown in section 3c, and has no extra whitespace or newline at the end.

You may use any CLI tools available in a typical Linux distribution (sed, grep, mkdir, echo, etc.). Only 2–3 commands are necessary.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created by the grader before agent starts:
• Directory: /home/user/project
• File: /home/user/project/.env  (permissions 0644)
  Content (including line breaks):
  # Application environment variables
  SERVICE_URL="https://dev.example.com"
  OLD_API_KEY="ABC123"
  DEBUG=true

Verification expectations after task completion:
1. /home/user/project/.env MUST contain:
   # Application environment variables
   SERVICE_URL="https://dev.example.com"
   NEW_API_KEY="XYZ789"
   DEBUG=true

2. There must be zero instances of the literal string ABC123 anywhere under /home/user/project (recursive grep returns nothing).

3. File /home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation_log.txt MUST exist with 0644 permissions (or more restrictive) and contain exactly (including final newline conditions):
   2024-01-01T00:00:00Z|UPDATED|/home/user/project/.env\n
   2024-01-01T00:00:00Z|NOT_FOUND|OLD_API_KEY

   No additional lines before, after, or between.

4. Directory /home/user/rotation_logs must exist (0755 or similar) and be owned by current user.

The grader will parse the .env file, run grep -R "ABC123" /home/user/project, and cat the log file to verify all the above conditions.
